reasoning: Bobby Finke broke the 1500m freestyle Long Course world record at the Paris Olympics on Aug 4, 2024. As of November 2025, he remains the record holder. Historically, the 1500m freestyle LC world record is broken very rarely (roughly once a decade: 2001, 2011, 2012, 2024) and almost exclusively during the summer peak season at the Olympics or World Championships. The period between November 2025 and February 2026 is the off-season for Long Course swimming (most winter meets are Short Course, e.g., National Championships and SC World Cups). The 2025 LC World Championships already concluded in July/August 2025 in Singapore. Thus, there are no major LC meets where elite swimmers would be fully tapered to challenge such a demanding record during this three-month window. The only small risks are a random national meet breakthrough or a retroactive doping invalidation, both of which are extremely unlikely (Finke's record is from over a year ago, so Olympic doping screens are mostly finalized). Taking the maximum allowed ceiling (0.95) for the nearest date and introducing slight uncertainty decay for distant dates.
